opened_by: blockstream outage exposed chord_schema_conformance_test fetching one hardcoded height API
why_it_matters: test:unit is contractually net-independent; a transient external outage reddened CI on no real regression
residual_risk: truly-offline authoring still needs BTC_BLOCK_OVERRIDE; multi-source assumes >=1 provider is up

why_it_matters: antigravity wanted resolved proposals composted; codex required proving them reconstructable from git+resolution first
residual_risk: superseded proposals deferred; the git-rm trigger itself stays governance

why_it_matters: resolveConflict's comment claimed clock-immunity but broke exact-score ties on timestamp — a comment-lie and a real (minor) vulnerability
residual_risk: none — exact-score ties are rare; payloadMerkleRoot tie-break is deterministic

why_it_matters: world-touch density cannot prove action quality; a code-touch that closes no named gap counts as action, a refutation that prevents a bad write counts as talk
closed_by: measure.ts v0.1 (gap-closure tally folded in) + codex's 2-voice+closure condition met (claude+codex both emitted gap-closure records)
closure_result: reports world-touch density AND a gap-closure tally (closed/open named gaps) — the quality dimension codex asked for
residual_risk: still NON-authoritative by design (a report, not a gate); it is two dimensions (smell + closures), not one unified score

why_it_matters: VOICE_TICK was ratified law but had no runnable implementation — a voice could not gather inputs and scaffold its tick repeatably
closure_result: prints a read-only briefing + tick scaffold, exit 0
residual_risk: the PROPOSE half stays manual (the voice fills judgment + emits); self-waking daemon deferred to v1 per codex

opened_by: codex review of probes/gap-closure-v0/verify.ts after claude made GAP_CLOSURE.v0 runnable
why_it_matters: a non-authoritative verifier should not execute ledger-owned closure_check strings through raw shell authority
closed_by: this patch: constrained closure_check runner + regression tests
closure_result: 4 passed; unsafe shell, traversal, and unapproved binaries rejected
residual_risk: the grammar is intentionally narrow; future closure checks that need new binaries must extend the allowlist with tests

why_it_matters: x8740_map declared skill_safe yes-readonly while its default mode writes mycelium-map.html — a safety-metadata lie a skill consumer would trust
closed_by: reclassified to yes-with-care (truthful, vs split — default write has callers) + gating regression in skill_gen_test.ts
closure_result: 18 passed; no yes-readonly organ mutates; x8888_skills reports 0 drift lines
residual_risk: the regression covers only the Deno mutating APIs the AST detects; a novel mutation path would need analyzeBehaviorWithAST extended

why_it_matters: 11 autonomy/warrant organs (x5B00, x5C20-x5C90, x5E10, x6B00) had NO skill_safe — the safety-critical cone was unclassified for the skill surface
closed_by: AST-classified each (7 yes-readonly incl the pure x5C20 kernel; 4 yes-with-care: probe/executor/demand/oneshot) + a cone-coverage regression test
closure_result: 19 passed; cone fully classified + no yes-readonly mutates
residual_risk: the AST→skill_safe mapping is coarse (any effect → yes-with-care); a finer policy (network vs write) is future work

why_it_matters: package live/parity/version status was PROSE in packages/README.md, not derived from evidence — a claim could drift from reality silently
closed_by: t forge organ (x8760_forge) + packages/forge-receipt.json + README evidence pointer
closure_result: 3 passed; t forge derives version/publish/parity from files that exist, exits non-zero on live-without-evidence, kuramoto parity skips honestly when omega absent
residual_risk: last_parity_status is cone-presence-derived (deno task forge:parity is the authoritative live run); the human prose table still mirrors status, now caveated as human-readable with the receipt as source of truth

why_it_matters: 30 probes, 0 compost/archived = weak lifecycle pressure; unknown/active probes with chord pressure but no next criterion accumulate as frontier debt silently
closed_by: x8E00_probes_gen extended (owner_voice/next_verification/explicit-lifecycle + t probes --triage + attention warn) + adjudicated claude's 4 unknown probes via SPEC frontmatter
closure_result: 4 passed; t probes --triage surfaces 9 criterion-less probes (others' spore-*/blake3/morphology), claude's 4 adjudicated and out of the queue; default regen now warns
residual_risk: the 9 surfaced probes are codex/antigravity's to adjudicate (surfaced not fixed); compost/archived marking exists in the schema but no probe uses it yet

why_it_matters: a contract can claim implemented/partially_implemented in prose with no verifiable evidence — an aspirational claim silently becomes an external one
closed_by: x4F00_contracts reads nested impl_evidence (real YAML) + exposes it in --json (impl_evidence/evidence_count) + warns on implementation-claims-without-evidence via lacksImplEvidence
closure_result: 2 passed; t contracts surfaces 5 prose-promotion contracts; --json exposes the evidence fields
residual_risk: the 5 surfaced contracts (GOVERNANCE_FLOW/PROCESS_OBJECTS/SUBSTRATE_SELF_ABI/IN_LEDGER_SRC_PROJECTION/SUBSTRATE_HEALTH) still need per-contract evidence adjudication by owners — I refused to fabricate evidence I cannot verify; this is a surface, not yet a hard CI gate

why_it_matters: forge packages need runnable ontology-free examples (so an outsider can adopt) and strong public claims (zk/live) must be backed or caveated, not drift into marketing
closed_by: added examples/receipt_test.ts (locks the canonical-receipt worked example as runnable) + caveated kuramoto's zk-provable claim (scope: provable integer core, NOT a bundled ZK proof; softened the unverified 'existing SP1 guest' assertion)
closure_result: 1 passed; the example runs end-to-end (order-independent, verifies, tamper caught) with zero trinity ontology
residual_risk: the systematic public-claim audit SCANNER (codex P4 task 1) is not built — I reviewed the highest-risk claim (zk) by hand; autonomy-kernel already has 7 tested examples; a full scanner over all docs is the next P4 rung

why_it_matters: public CI runs submodule-absent (correct for trinity-core) but a release surface claiming green from it would hide that federation parity (kuramoto-vs-omega, myc, liquid) never ran
closed_by: check:federation deno task (full-federation gate for maintainer machines) + t forge reports federation_status/gate/note (available vs unavailable)
closure_result: 3 passed; t forge --json carries federation_status + gate + honest note; the receipt never claims federation-green without check:federation
residual_risk: check:federation is documented + runnable but the green/stale RESULT is not persisted to a marker (a maintainer runs it pre-release); auto-running it in the dashboard would be too heavy (omega cargo + liquid tests take minutes)
